title: Fix internvl fp16 error
body:

step_plan.summary: Fixes InternVL/InternVL2 runtime failures when running in FP16 on Intel XPU by making fragile ops (pos-embed resize, vision tensor handling, and attention) use safe dtype/device pathways and adding fallbacks when fp16 XPU kernels are unsupported.

steps.operation: Make positional-embedding resize explicitly FP32-on-CPU and return tensor in the model’s target dtype/device safely
steps.reason: Bicubic `interpolate` is a common unsupported/unstable op for FP16 on XPU; forcing the resize to run in float32 on CPU (or otherwise a known-safe dtype/device) and only then casting/moving back prevents FP16 kernel errors and avoids dtype/device mismatch when the embedding is later added to hidden states.
steps.side_effects: May introduce a CPU round-trip during pos-embed resize (typically only when image size/pos-embed size changes); must ensure returned dtype matches the downstream hidden-state dtype to avoid implicit casts; should not regress BF16/FP32 or CPU paths.

steps.operation: Harden single-chat input tensor preparation: keep text tensors on device, and gate/correct pixel_values dtype for FP16-on-XPU
steps.reason: Reported InternVL/InternVL2 failures in FP16 often come from casting `pixel_values` to fp16 on XPU when the vision tower or pre/post-processing ops expect fp32 (or when fp16 kernels are missing). Add logic to keep `pixel_values` in a safe dtype (e.g., fp32) for the vision path while still allowing the language model weights to be fp16, and ensure all tensors are on consistent devices.
steps.side_effects: Slightly higher memory/bandwidth for vision tensors if kept in fp32; may change numerics minimally; must ensure no API behavior change besides improved stability (chat output should remain consistent).

steps.operation: Apply the same dtype/device guardrails for batched chat, including consistent mask/padding handling and per-sample pixel batch dtype
steps.reason: The batched path frequently diverges from single-sample handling and can surface dtype/device mismatches sooner (e.g., attention masks on CPU, pixel batch cast to fp16). Mirror the safe-casting and device-move rules from `internvl_chat` so FP16-on-XPU works for batch inference too.
steps.side_effects: Potential behavior change for users who relied on implicit casting of `pixel_values` to `self.dtype`; batch memory may increase if using fp32 vision inputs; requires validating both `pixel_values=None` warmup and real multimodal batches.

steps.operation: Add FP16-on-XPU attention safety: upcast softmax/attention math or force a supported fallback when SDP kernels are unsupported
steps.reason: If the runtime error is coming from scaled-dot-product attention kernel selection on XPU for fp16, this is the chokepoint to (a) force a safer compute dtype (e.g., fp32 for logits/softmax) while keeping outputs in fp16, or (b) disable/override the SDP path when fp16+xpu is detected and fall back to a known-supported attention implementation.
steps.side_effects: May impact performance for fp16 attention on XPU (fallback or upcast costs); must preserve correctness and not regress bf16/fp32; could affect attention dropout behavior if different kernel path is chosen, so downstream generation should be sanity-checked.
